channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Punjab Minister Arrested, CBI Probe Demanded in Suicide Case
item.description: The situation in Punjab is quite tense, boss! After Punjab minister Laljit Singh Bhullar was arrested in connection with an abetment-to-suicide case, the opposition parties – BJP, Congress, and Akali ...

item.title: Temporary Evacuation at Newark Airport After Burning Smell Detected
item.description: Operations at Newark Liberty International Airport in New Jersey were temporarily halted on Monday morning after air traffic controllers evacuated the control tower due to a reported burning smell. Th...

item.title: Lipa Archbishop Urges Faithful to Embrace Simple Living and Solidarity Amid Rising Costs from Middle East Conflict

item.title: The Role of Language in Shaping the US-Iran Conflict
item.description: The longstanding conflict between the United States, its ally Israel, and Iran has been influenced not only by strategic and political factors but also by the language used to describe each other over...

item.title: PM Modi Alerts on Internal Security Risks from West Asia Conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi warned in the Rajya Sabha about potential threats to India's internal security amid the ongoing West Asia conflict, which is disrupting energy supplies. He highlighted tha...

item.title: Prolonged Detention of Nigerian Military Officers Accused of Coup Plot Raises Human Rights Concerns
item.description: Na serious matter wey dey happen for Naija military side. Some officers wey dem arrest between late September and early October last year for alleged coup plot against President Bola Tinubu government...

item.title: UN Warns Earth Is Approaching Critical Climate Thresholds
item.description: The United Nations has declared that the Earth's climate is in a 'state of emergency,' citing unprecedented imbalances and escalating risks. The World Meteorological Organization (WMO) reports that fr...

item.title: How striped mice adapt to drought by conserving energy and staying calm
item.description: For the arid Succulent Karoo region of South Africa, striped mice (Rhabdomys pumilio) don show scientists say small animals fit survive long drought periods without too much stress. Instead of getting...

item.title: Rural road neglect in South Africa deepens inequality and limits access to basic rights
item.description: In South Africa’s rural areas, particularly in the Eastern Cape, many people depend on poorly maintained gravel roads to reach schools, hospitals, and workplaces. When these roads deteriorate or wash ...

item.description: The Tanzania Meteorological Authority (TMA) has called on Tanzanians to closely follow weather forecasts and make use of accurate climate information as the rainy season begins across different parts ...

item.description: The 2026 Assam Assembly elections are shaping up to be a heated and competitive affair, with All India United Democratic Front (AIUDF) chief Badruddin Ajmal positioning himself for a political comebac...

item.title: SOM Designs Mountain-Inspired Towers for Kazakhstan’s New Planned City
item.description: The architectural firm Skidmore, Owings & Merrill (SOM) has unveiled plans for a striking new urban center in Kazakhstan, inspired by the surrounding Trans-Ili Alatau mountains. Known as Alatau, this ...

item.title: Audit Finds Weak Oversight in Canada’s International Student Program
item.description: A new report from Auditor General Karen Hogan reveals major shortcomings in the federal oversight of Canada’s International Student Program. The audit highlights critical weaknesses in how Immigration...

item.title: African Green Bank Proposed to Boost Renewable Energy and Industrialization
item.description: Make una hear dis one well well. Dis article talk about how Africa dey suffer from climate change, even though we no contribute much to the problem. Things like flood, heat, and dry season dey make li...

item.title: Pakistan Plays Key Role in Back-Channel Diplomacy to Ease US-Iran Tensions
item.description: According to a report by the Financial Times, Pakistan has emerged as the leading mediator in efforts to resolve the ongoing conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran. Chief of Defence Forc...

item.title: Njugush’s Comment on Celestine Ndinda’s Post Sparks Reactions Amid Ongoing Breakup Speculation

item.title: Ekiti State to use arts and culture to boost voter participation
item.description: Ekiti State Government don announce say dem go use arts and culture take educate people about the need to avoid voter apathy and make sure dem vote correctly for the June 20 governorship election. Pro...

item.title: Nhulunbuy faces food security risks as Woolworths announces closure of its only major supermarket by mid-2027
item.description: Residents of Nhulunbuy in East Arnhem Land, one of Australia's most remote regions, have expressed serious concern following Woolworths' announcement that its Gove supermarket will close at the end of...
